title: beef noodle soup
creator: Unknown
description: Kuaitiao nuea pueay (Thai script: ก๋วยเตี๋ยวเนื้อเปื่อย) is a noodle soup with tender braised beef. It is the Thai version of the Chinese "braised beef noodles" (Chinese script: (紅燒牛肉麵). This particular version is with sen yai noodles, also known as shahe fen in Chinese. Condiments for this dish are roasted dried chilli flakes, and a dip made with mashed green bird's eye chillies, ginger, garlic, sugar and vinegar.

title: Cloisonné incense burners
creator: Unknown
description: These extravagant perfumers, commissioned by the Qianlong emperor, reference the ritual tripods of the Shang and Zhou dynasties but use a palette of Qing colours. Both feature traditional Chinese emblems associated with long life, such as deer. Delicate-looking cranes, symbols of immortality, support the heavy burners, which would originally have graced an official room within the palace alongside other cloisonné items. Oing dynasty Qianlong period, 1736-95 Beijing, China Donated by Sir H.S.E. Vanderpant. Picture taken at the British Museum, London, UK.

title: Filled-in Gold and Lacquered Dragon Throne 戧金及填漆龍紋寶座, Grand Lisboa Palace
creator: Unknown
file: Filled-in Gold and Lacquered Dragon Throne 戧金及填漆龍紋寶座, Grand Lisboa Palace.jpg
description: According to the exhibition placard: This five-panel lacquered throne was an imperial commission for Emperor Kangxi of the Qing dynasty. It is the only known surviving throne to incorporate two traditional decorative arts of qiangjin (incised gold) and tianqi (filled-in lacquer) techniques. Softened by arched corners, the throne's panels are richly adorned with carved and inlaid motifs of crashing waves, swirling clouds, and imperial dragons. The seat itself features decorative border and cloud patterns, further embellished with blooming flowers.

title: Jade Cong from China, Neolithic Period
creator: Unknown
file: Jade Cong from China, Neolithic Period.jpg
description: "Among Neolithic jades, the cong displays the most complex form: a cylindrical tube encased in a square prism that gently tapers from top to bottom. Simple masks with circular eyes and bar-shaped mouths decorate the corners. Enigmatic in function and meaning, the cong probably signified wealth and social status. In a tomb found at Sidun, Jiangsu Province, numerous cong lay in a circle around the dead, suggesting that they also served a religious or ritual purpose." (Metropolitan Museum of Art object description)

title: Jade disc (Bi), a flat circular slab of jade with a central hole
creator: Unknown
file: Jade disc (Bi). Nephrite jade. Liangzhu Culture, south-east China. Neolithic period, c. 2500 BCE. Victoria and Albert Museum, London.jpg
description: Jade disc (Bi), a flat circular slab of jade with a central hole. An ancient Chinese text entitled the "Zhouli" (Rituals of Zhou) describes the "bi" as a ceremonial object to sacrifice to Heaven. The precise significance is unknown; jade discs were found in many graves and might have had a ritual role. Nephrite jade, variegated dark green and brown. Liangzhu Culture, south-east China. Neolithic period, c. 2500 BCE. Victoria and Albert Museum, London.

title: The Daoist Immortal Han Xiangzi
creator: Liu Jun
file: Han Xiangzi, by Ming court painter Liu Jun.jpg
description: This painting depicts the Daoist immortal Han Xiangzi (韓湘子) miraculously treading the surface of a turbulent sea and playing a tune with a bamboo flute. The immortal wears a rustic cape of sawtoothed leaves and curly vegetal tendrils, pants with a skirt of reeds, a satchel lined with animal fur over his shoulder, and a belt holding two dried gourds.

title: Lacquer buddha
creator: Unknown
description: Buddha, probably Amitabha (Amituo). Buddhist images executed in dry lacquer were highly valued by the Chinese because of their costly and time-consuming process of production. There are so few surviving examples that this seated Buddha is especially precious. To fashion the body of the image, the craftsman made a rough form of the sculpture in clay and then applied at least three layers of hemp cloth, each secured with a paste made of raw lacquer (the sap from the lac tree, Rhus verniciflua ) and a fine powder of bone, horn, shell, ceramic, stone, or …

He was posthumously honored with the status of a Guanyin, the Bodhisattva of Mercy. He became a cult figure during the Song Dynasty and was believed to prevent floods if prayed to.

title: Western Han Chinese Silk
creator: Unknown
description: This 6¾ foot long Western Han painting on silk was found draped over the coffin in the grave of Lady Dai (c. 168 BC) at Mawangdui near Changsha in Hunan province, China. The scenes depicted on it seem to illustrate the journey of the woman's soul. The top section shows the heavenly realm, complete with dragons, leopards, and hybrid creatures. At the corners are the crow that symbolizes the sun and the toad that symbolizes the moon, the pairing of the sun and moon representing the cosmic forces of yin and yang.

description: The design of this Central Asian tapestry is a synthesis of Western and Chinese elements. Bands of pearls and split palmettes and the deer with mushroom-shaped antlers originated in Iran and Sogdiana but migrated to China during the Tang dynasty (618-907). Although these motifs disappeared from Chinese art after the fall of the Tang, they survived in Central Asia for centuries. The dragon is Chinese in origin, but has a Central Asian form (particularly its extended snout). Also characteristic of Central Asia are the placement of the dragon among flowe…

title: Each warrior had a different face
creator: Unknown
file: Each warrior had a different face (35687732155).jpg
credit: shankar s. from Dubai, united arab emirates
description: Every figure in the Terracotta warriors army differs in facial features and expression, clothing, hairstyle, and gestures, providing abundant and detailed artifacts for the study of the military, cultural, and economic history of that period. Some skeptics were wondering if the king had killed off his army just before his death which resulted the face of each individual to be copied. Closer scrutiny has however shown that there are basically ten kinds of faces, which were used round and round and in an assorted fashion. More on this in a later caption…

title: Lush greenery around the Terracotta warriors pit
creator: Unknown
file: Lush greenery around the Terracotta warriors pit (35648438506).jpg
credit: shankar s. from Dubai, united arab emirates
description: Tickets in hand, meeting one of the farmers who discovered the buried terracotta figures and gift purchases over, it was now time to move towards the excavation site. It's a short walk, and by this time it had started drizzling pretty heavily. There are three excavation pits in all, of which we will be exploring one. (I think it was Pit # 1). Here we are, my guide Alena and I, walking towards the Pit # 1. Some lush greenery here. (Xi'an, Shaanxi, China, May 2017)
